title: A Brave Actor's Story
summary: Michael Campbell was an actor from Belfast who had a serious illness called motor neurone disease (MND). He played the role of Richard III in a wheelchair and won a big drama award for his amazing performance. He was 35 years old when he passed away. His wife Naomi and friends said he was very inspiring and full of joy. The Lyric Theatre in Belfast, where he performed, shared how sad they are. Michael showed great strength and made people happy with his acting and storytelling. He will be remembered as a talented and brave person who touched many lives.

background_read: Michael Campbell was an actor from Belfast who faced a tough illness called motor neurone disease. He didn't let it stop him from acting and even performed as Richard III in a wheelchair, winning a major award. His story shows how people can be brave and inspire others even during hard times. Learning about actors and illnesses can help us understand how art and health connect in our lives.
Article_Structure: WHO: Michael Campbell, an actor from Belfast with motor neurone disease
WHAT: He played Richard III in a wheelchair, won a big award, and passed away at age 35
WHERE: He performed at the Lyric Theatre in Belfast and lived there
WHY: To share his inspiring story of strength and talent despite his illness

perspectives.description: Michael's wife and friends remember him as joyful and inspiring
Theatres praised his talent and resilience in acting
